title: Dathka Graush
content:
{{Top|pfa|leg}} {{FAreview}} {{Character |type=Sith |image= |name=Dathka Graush |homeworld=[[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031002korriban|text=Korriban: Planet of Lost Souls|int=Korriban: Planet of Lost Souls}} |birth= |death=c. [[6950 BBY]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' says that Dathka Graush initiated his war in [[7000 BBY]] and Korriban: Planet of Lost Souls says that he was assassinated "after fifty years of bloodshed". |species=[[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] |gender= |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=[[Graush dynasty]] |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices=[[Cronal]]''[[Luke Skywalker and the Shadows of Mindor]]'' }} {{Quote|… there? Come out, I'm armed. Step into the light, damn it… Yeah? What makes you think you have anything I want?"
«It is done. I am free. Wake up, there is work to do.»|quoteend=0|Captain Naz Felyood awakens and is possessed by the long-dead spirit of Dathka Graush, who, upon re-awakening, begins to order his Korriban zombies|}} '''Dathka Graush''' was a [[Sith sorcerer]] who lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] more than seven thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Brutality and trepidation were the tools Graush used to defeat his enemies during one of Korriban's recurrent civil wars, the end of which saw him acclaimed [[Sith King|king]] of his [[Sith (species)/Legends|species]]. In the war's wake, Graush made use of both [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith sorcery]] and [[Sith alchemy/Legends|alchemy]] to perfect the [[Tsaiwinokka Hoyakut]] spell, through which he resurrected an [[Army/Legends|army]] of [[Korriban zombie|reanimated corpses]] bound to him through the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. He also had his [[Heart/Legends|heart]] removed and replaced with a magical [[Heart of Graush|crystal]] that was imbued with the power of [[The Force/Legends|the Force's]] malevolent side. The [[Graush dynasty]] ultimately culminated with his [[Death/Legends|death]] at the hands of [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]], and the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] King was [[Dathka Graush's tomb|interred]] within Korriban's [[Valley of Golg]]. Sometime between the destruction of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] and the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of the long-dead Sith King was disturbed after millennia of slumber. [[Cronal]], a [[Prophets of the Dark Side|dark side prophet]] formerly in the service of his [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Majesty]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], forcefully sundered Graush's ghost from his Korriban grave and compelled the Sith King to reveal the most potent of his alchemical secrets. The knowledge Graush imparted to Cronal assisted the latter in the creation and manipulation of the electromagnetically-charged [[meltmassif]] [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]]. ==Biography== ===Rise to power=== [[File:Moraband-FDCR.png|thumb|left|250px|Korriban, homeworld of Dathka Graush]] A pure-[[Blood/Legends|blooded]] [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], Dathka Graush lived in the especially tumultuous era between the [[Subterra Period|Subterra]] and [[Manderon Period|Manderon]] periods of [[Timeline of galactic history/Legends|galactic history]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' Graush's was a life thrust into the commonplace violence of the indigenous [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]''[[Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties]]'' states that all Sith post–27,700 BBY were part of a society consumed by civil war; ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' states that all Sith grew up in a violent and warlike society. as civilization on Korriban degenerated into a primitive state of perpetual [[Korriban civil war|war]] in the millennia following the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Sith King]] [[Adas]] nearly [[27,700 BBY|twenty thousand years prior]]. With none to justly assume the mantle, many Sith declared themselves ''[[Sith'ari/Legends|Sith'ari]]'', or [[overlord]] in the [[Sith (language)|native tongue]], as tradition had dictated since Adas's passing. Such was the civil strife that Sith society as a whole was forced to relocate to the nearby world of [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]],{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}} while those who remained on Korriban became religious cultists. Regardless of the controvertible affirmations of authority made by various Sith who felt entitled to the throne, commonality on Korriban remained utterly fragmented as individual Sith vied for worldwide domination. It was during that [[Time/Legends|time]] when Dathka Graush earned the reputation of a fierce conqueror. In [[7000 BBY]], he and his forces championed a [[Korriban civil war (Dathka Graush)|civil war]] that resulted in the destruction of the majority of Korriban's population centers. Graush emerged victorious and was crowned King of the Sith. At the height of his power, Graush built a [[Dathka Graush's fortress|fortress]] in the [[Naos Asteroid Belt]], where he would store powerful artifacts.{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Archaeology|Legacy of Dathka Graush}} During his reign, Graush expanded his reach across Korriban's territories until two-thirds of the planet submitted to his rule. Graush dominated his kinsfolk in a manner that was considered extremely brutal even by Sith standards, and his endeavors of bloodshed and terror saw countless corpses strewn across the battlefield in his wake. Graush took interest in the abundance of cadaverous material, and with ancient [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith sorcery]] and [[Sith alchemy/Legends|alchemy]] as his means, he performed the [[Tsaiwinokka Hoyakut]], translated from ancient Sith as the "Reanimated Dead" spell,''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'' whereby he resurrected an [[Army/Legends|army]] of [[Korriban zombie]]s. The entities were [[Zombie/Legends|undead]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]], whose plight was spread to other [[Sentience/Legends|sentient beings]] through their bite. [[File:Heart_of_Graush.png|thumb|right|200px|The Heart of Graush]] In an effort to sustain himself through unnatural means, Graush had fashioned for himself a [[Heart of Graush|crystalline jewel]]{{CSWECite|II|29|Heart of Graush}} filled with [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Force ghost/Legends|spirits]] that had been collected over the millennia. Using both Sith sorcery and the most advanced technology of the day, Graush replaced his natural-born [[Heart/Legends|heart]] with the [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]]. Despite the belief of some that the crystal made Graush invincible, he fell victim to [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] after only [[6950 BBY|fifty years]] of rule. With his death, the spirits of his reanimated zombie army were wrenched from their bodies and made to join those already trapped within Graush's crystal heart.{{CSWECite|II|227|Korriban zombie}} ===Legacy=== ====The Old Republic==== An [[Dathka Graush's tomb|elaborate tomb]] was constructed for Dathka Graush in Korriban's [[Valley of Golg]]. Located near Korriban's equator, the millennia-old tomb metropolis at Golg was one of the many "Valleys of the Sith [[Lord/Legends|Lords]]" scattered across the planet. Graush's and the other [[Burial/Legends|tombs]] that were erected in the Valley were lined with stonework carvings and [[Sith runes|runes]] of protective Sith sorcery. The late King's personal crypt was in a part of the Valley utterly devoid of [[Star/Legends|sunlight]], within a large cavernous gallery, wherein the shrines and respective pictograms of fourteen [[Sith sorcerer]]s were kept. The largest shrine of all contained [[Dathka Graush's sarcophagus|Graush's sarcophagus]], where his actual remains had been entombed with a [[Sith sword|sword]], an [[Sith talisman/Legends|amulet]], [[Dathka Graush's holocron|his holocron]], and his crystalline heart. In [[6900 BBY]], the [[Exiles]], [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] survivors of the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]], a rebellion against their [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]] counterparts in the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], conquered [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith space]] and became the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Lords of the Sith]].[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] Among their number was Sith alchemist [[Sorzus Syn]], who discovered and learned from the Heart of Graush to create a Sith amulet of her own, the [[Muur Talisman]]. Syn recorded the history of the Heart and her experimentation with the crystal in a journal, pages of which were recovered and kept in the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]] before being compiled into the ''[[Book of Sith]]'' by Sith Lord and [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. [[File:Helm.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Helm of Dathka Graush]] In [[3963 BBY]],The events of [[Knights of the Old Republic 29|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 29]] take place 3,963 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], 3,963 years after [[3963 BBY]]. a clandestine subsect of supposed lightsiders known as the [[Jedi Covenant]] undertook the task of collecting and quarantining all known [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]], with hopes of both preventing the return of the Sith, as well as the spread of dark side influence through their treasures. Among them was the [[Helm of Dathka Graush]], a battle-headdress thought to have once been worn by the ancient Sith King. Graush's device considerably amplified the [[Force power/Legends|Force abilities]] of its wearer, and was also believed to strengthen [[Sword of Ieldis|the sword]] of the ancient Sith Lord [[Ieldis]]. The power of Graush's helm was also such that, when removed from the protective [[nullification resin]], its residual energies caused significant [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmospheric]] disturbances within a six-[[Kilometer/Legends|kilometer]] radius of the planet [[Odryn]]'s [[Sanctum of the Exalted]].[[Knights of the Old Republic 29|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 29]] Around [[3643 BBY]],Act I of ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' takes place around [[3643 BBY]] per the reasoning [[Template:TOR dates|here]]. in the final days of the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]], an archeological crew travelled to the Naos Asteroid Belt and rediscovered the millennia-old fortress of Dathka Graush. The lost vaults, which had remained untrespassed for centuries, were opened, and a series of precious artifact fragments were salvaged. ====The Imperial Era==== {{Quote|Dathka, my friend. Dathka, save me. I did as you asked. I called the lost ones, and set them on the betrayers. Dathka, my friend. Where did you go? Did you leave with the lost ones? They took your Heart. Took it from me, and now I cannot hear you. Did I displease you? The lost ones don't know you. How can they? I must find the ones who survived. They have your power; they are survivors. Like I am a survivor. I must find you, Dathka, my friend. We will be strong again. We will feast on their hearts, and the blood of the weak shall wash pure the dead stone. They destroyed virtue, and the lost ones are scattered. But you are out there, Dathka. Dathka, my friend. Dathka, our master.|Captain Naz Felyood, compelled by the power of Dathka Graush's spirit|}} Exactly [[1 BBY/Legends|six months]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destruction]] of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]], [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Naz Felyood]] crashed his [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''[[Jynni's Virtue]]'', on Korriban after it had been fired upon by [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaked]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] ships and a subsequent [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] malfunction. The ''Virtue'' landed two kilometers north of the Valley of Golg, and the crew was forced to stay on Korriban longer than they had originally intended. They hoped that the faint power emanations they had detected from the Valley was enough to revitalize the ''Virtue'', but in actuality the faint energy signatures that had interfered with their electrical systems were the result of the Valley's lingering dark side power. Nevertheless, Felyood entered the Valley himself and found his way into Graush's resting place. The starship captain infiltrated the crypt, singled out Graush's shrine, and planned to raid it first. As he worked to liberate Graush's sarcophagus of its treasures, the long-dead King's spirit appeared and tried to convince Felyood that he had something the pirate wanted before finally possessing him. [[File:Tsaiwinokka_hoyakut-BOS.png|thumb|right|300px|Dathka Graush's tomb was protected by Sith undead.]] Graush also resurrected his undead army, whose ravenous hunger for living flesh sent them after the ''Jynni's Virtue''{{'s}} remaining crewmembers. The zombies spread their plight throughout the crew, but in a final act of despair, the uninfected crewmembers committed suicide through the activation of their ship's [[Self-destruct mechanism/Legends|self-destruct mechanism]]. The resultant explosion vaporized a large portion of the army that Graush had loosed upon the crew. What remained of Graush's zombies took the crystalline heart from Felyood, and Graush's shade left with it. Felyood, now alone and undying, was compelled by the power of Graush's heart and searched the expanse of Korriban for its master for all eternity. In the period between the Battle of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]] and the Empire's [[Battle of Endor/Legends|defeat]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], the former [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]] known as Lord [[Cronal]] devoted his life to the study of ancient and forgotten knowledge, such as that of the ancient Sith and other supposed masters of what the [[Sorcerers of Rhand]] called [[Way of the Dark|the Dark]]. Following his [[Darksight]] visions to numerous places around [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in his quest for ancient Sith lore, Cronal was led to Dathka Graush's spirit in the Valley of Golg. With knowledge he obtained from the [[Temple of Korman Lao]], Cronal captured Graush's spirit and forced the ancient Sith King to relinquish his most arcane alchemical secrets. From Graush's teachings, Cronal constructed his [[Shadow Crown|Sunset Crown]], a device he used to control the electrochemical crystalline [[meltmassif]] substance of the planet [[Mindor/Legends|Mindor]]. Eventually, trusted [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] researchers of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|Jedi Academy]] were sent to the Sith [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Korriban and came across Graush's tomb. There they retrieved a set of recordings that had originally belonged to the crew of the ''Jynni's Virtue'', which spoke of the events leading to Graush's awakening [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before. A brief written message showing the last known words of Captain Felyood was also found with the tapes. Even though the tapes were dismissed as frauds by some [[Historian/Legends|historians]] and Jedi, enlightenment from them led Jedi researchers to attempt to locate Graush's crystal heart. At a great risk to their own lives, surveyors were sent into Graush's tomb to find the Sith King's heart, but they found nothing despite a thorough survey of his crypt. ==Personality and traits== [[File:ThreeSith-BOS.png|thumb|left|300px|Graush belonged to the Sith species.]] Dathka Graush was a pure-blooded member of the Sith [[Species/Legends|species]]. Sith like Graush typically were known for their glowing [[Color/Legends|yellow]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], crimson-hued skin, sharp predatory features over their bodies,''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' and [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]], especially attuned to the dark side of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' Graush's fifty-year reign and the acts that ultimately resulted in his rise to power, such as the mass accumulation of corpses and devastation of Korriban's [[City/Legends|cities]], left him remembered as a bloodthirsty conqueror and a tyrant. The acts of terror he committed over the people he ruled were considered cruel even by Sith standards—a species that placed acts of violence, war, sentient sacrifice, and blood consumption on par with peace. Such behavior was dubiously attributed to the absence of his biological heart, after he replaced it with a Force-sensitive crystal. ==Power and abilities== [[File:ShadowSpawnZoom.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Cronal built on Graush's teachings to create the Shadow Crown.]] With his personal Sith [[Sword/Legends|sword]], Dathka Graush proved himself as a reckoning force on the battlefield. His tactics helped him to emerge victorious over his enemies, and those who survived the war of his instigation became his subjects in its aftermath. A powerful Sith sorcerer and alchemist as well, Graush's Force talents and [[Scientist/Legends|scientific]] aptitude served him in both the creation of his Korriban zombies and cardiac transplantation procedure. Graush's facility with Sith alchemy was legendary, and he was credited as the pioneer of the "Reanimated Dead" spell, Tsaiwinokka Hoyakut. The Sorcerer of Rhand Cronal believed the ancient Sith had little understanding of the Dark and destruction but reasoned that accomplishments like those of Graush's were impressive nonetheless. He concluded that were he to study and pursue Graush's ancient lore himself, he would be able to achieve more through whatever enlightenment he gained. It was through Graush's knowledge that Lord Cronal learned to construct his dark side–oriented devices called Shadow Crowns. ==Behind the scenes== Dathka Graush received his first mention in ''[[Korriban: Planet of Lost Souls]]'', a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] supplement written by [[Cory J. Herndon]] that was first published [[Wizards.com|online]] on [[October 2]], [[2003]] by [[Wizards of the Coast]]. ''Korriban: Planet of Lost Souls'', which was published when the [[canon]] was that the consecration of the first [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] happened in ~[[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' refers to Dathka Graush as a "pureblooded Sith Dark Lord" who ruled over two-thirds of Korriban before 6950 BBY. However, ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] the consecration of the first Dark Lord of the Sith to have happened in 6900 BBY,''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' fifty years after Graush's death. ''[[Luke Skywalker and the Shadows of Mindor]]'', a [[2008]] novel by [[Matthew Stover]], identifies Dathka Graush as a "King of the Sith". [[File:Graush_beheaded.png|thumb|left|250px|Hakagram Graush was created for ''Book of Sith'' to take Dathka Graush's place.]] Graush received a fleeting mention, albeit with his name misspelled as "Grausch," in [[Knights of the Old Republic 29|the twenty-ninth issue]] of the [[Dark Horse Comics]] series [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']], written by [[John Jackson Miller]] and published on [[May 29]], 2008.{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54348/star_wars_knights_of_the_old_republic_2006_29|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (2006) #29}} Graush was later referenced in the [[2012]] publication ''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'', by [[Daniel Wallace]]. The book also included an illustration of the Dark Jedi Exile [[Ajunta Pall]] beheading the Sith king [[Hakagram Graush]]. Wallace originally intended for Dathka Graush to be the beheaded king. However, after realizing that that would not fit with Dathka's previously-established timeline, Wallace introduced Hakagram instead, as a probable descendant of Dathka.{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2012/03/endnotes-for-star-wars-book-of-sith_15.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for Star Wars: Book of Sith (part 2)}} ==Appearances== *[[Knights of the Old Republic 29|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 29]] {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Luke Skywalker and the Shadows of Mindor]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031002korriban|text=Korriban: Planet of Lost Souls|int=Korriban: Planet of Lost Souls}} {{1stm}} *{{CSWECite|multiple}} *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=68456&month=092009|text=Friday Feature – ''Death Troopers'' Sneak Preview}} *''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'' {{StartBox}} {{SuccessionBox |title=[[Sith King|King]] of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] {{C|concurrent with other numerous self-proclaimed Sith [[King/Legends|Kings]]}} |years=c. [[7000 BBY]] – c. [[6950 BBY]] |before=Eventually King [[Adas]] |before-years=c. [[28,000 BBY]] – c. [[27,700 BBY]] |after=Eventually [[Hakagram Graush]] |after-years=—c. [[6900 BBY]]}} {{EndBox}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Dathka Graush |es=Dathka Graush |fi=Dathka Graush |fr=Dathka Graush |it=Dathka Graush |pl=Dathka Graush }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Graush, Dathka}} [[Category:Force ghosts]] [[Category:Sith alchemists]] [[Category:Sith kings]] [[Category:Sith Masters]] [[Category:Sith sorcerers]] [[Category:Sith species]]
